whatItIs: In Michigan, a name change is normally published and becomes a public court record. A court can waive publication and keep the record confidential for good cause — including a risk to the petitioner's safety.
actions: When you file, ask the court to waive publication and keep the record confidential for good cause on safety grounds — a legal-aid or LGBTQ legal org can help you show good cause.
If your order is already public, ask the clerk what options exist to restrict it after the fact.
Then suppress the downstream copies: broker opt-outs, Google 'Results about you', and the web-archive and search-cache removals.
disclaimer: Informational only — not legal advice, and not yet reviewed by a lawyer. Michigan practice varies by county and judge and changes over time; verify with the court or a legal-aid org before filing.
